compiled_state.title: [suite-engine.sh]: failing test file with many assertions -> CI log tail -30 truncates earlier FAIL: lines, hiding root cause
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Deferred Review Nitpick

#### Expected Behavior

When a hook test file fails (e.g., \`test-record-test-status-centrality.sh\` with 26 assertions, 2 failing), the CI log should show the failing assertion names and their \`FAIL:\` output so an engineer can identify root cause from the log alone.

#### Actual Behavior

\`tests/lib/suite-engine.sh\` line 594 uses \`tail -30\` when dumping failed test output, which can truncate all \`FAIL:\` lines when they occur early in a long test file. Observed this session: the 2 failing assertions in \`test_missing_sg_gracefully_degrades\` occurred before line 565 of the test file, but \`tail -30\` only captured PASS lines from tests after that point. The log showed 24 PASS / 2 FAIL counts but no FAIL line text — root cause was only identifiable by reproducing in a local Ubuntu container.

### 3. Technical Environment

* File: \`tests/lib/suite-engine.sh\` line 594
* Relevant context lines:
  \`\`\`
  # Limit to last 30 lines to avoid flooding CI logs
  tail -30 "$results_dir/$ftname.out"
  \`\`\`

### 4. Action History

* Observation only. Worked around by running the test in an Ubuntu docker container locally to capture full output.

### 5. Investigation Pointers (non-speculative)

Possible improvements (not proposing a specific fix):
* Filter output to just \`FAIL:\` lines + surrounding context, rather than \`tail -30\`.
* Print the last 30 lines PLUS all lines matching \`^FAIL:\`.
* Preserve the full \`.out\` file as a CI artifact so engineers can download it when the tail is insufficient.
